channel.title: Posts on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on mahmood

item.title: calculus
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) its important to read the exercises provided on every subject because i explain important things with exercises 1 factoring formulas \begin{align} a^2-b^2 &= (a-b)(a+b)\\ (a+b)^2 &= a^2+2ab+b^2\\ (a-b)^2 &= a^2-2ab+b^2\\ ax^2+bx+c &= a(x-x_1)(x-x_2)\\ a^3-b^3 &= (a-b)(a^2+b^2+ab)\\ a^3+b^3 &= (a+b)(a^2-ab+b^2)\\ (a-b)^3 &= a^3-b^3-3ab(a-b)\\ (a+b)^3 &= a^3+b^3+3ab(a+b)\\ (a+b+c)^3 &= a^3+b^3+c^3+3(a+b)(b+c)(c+a)\\ \frac{1}{k(k+1)} &= \frac1k - \frac{1}{k+1} \end{align} 2 trigonometric formulas \begin{align} \sin^2{x} + \cos^2{x} &= 1\\ \cos 2x &= 2\cos^2 x - 1\\ \cos 2x &= 1 - 2\sin^2 x\\ \cos 2x &= \cos^2 x - \sin^2 x\\ \sin 2x &= 2\sin x \cos x\\ 1 + \tan^2 x &= \frac{1}{\cos^2 x}\\ 1 + \cot^2 x &= \frac{1}{\sin^2 x} \end{align}

item.title: sagemath
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) video demonstrating sagemath+org-mode workflow: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cd4_O3TqArs sagemath is an open source alternative to Mathematica written in python, it preparses python code to allow for extra syntactic sugars its basically a python interface to a collection of mathematical software like Maxima/sympy 1 plotting 1.1 area below functions interval = [1, 8.5] interval_fill = [2, 8] f(x) = (x-3)*(x-5)*(x-7)+40 import os.path try: _ = open_left except: open_left = False try: _ = open_right except: open_right = False if not open_left: my_plot = line([(interval_fill[0],0),(interval_fill[0],f(interval_fill[0]))], color='black') if not open_right: my_plot += line([(interval_fill[1], 0),(interval_fill[1], f(interval_fill[1]))], color='black') my_plot += plot(f, (interval_fill[0], interval_fill[1]), fill=True) + plot(f, (interval[0], interval[1]), thickness=2) my_plot.

item.title: networking
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) this document is still being constructed, it should not be used for reference yet. 1 storage units basic units table: unit description bit 1 or 0 byte 8 bits kilobyte 1024 bytes megabyte 1024 kilobytes gigabyte 1024 megabytes terabyte 1024 gigabytes petabyte 1024 terabytes 2 unit prefix table prefix exponent yocto \(10^{-24}\) zepto \(10^{-21}\) atto \(10^{-18}\) femto \(10^{-15}\) pico \(10^{-12}\) nano \(10^{-9}\) micro \(10^{-6}\) milli \(10^{-3}\) kilo \(10^3\) mega \(10^6\) giga \(10^9\) tera \(10^{12}\) peta \(10^{15}\) exa \(10^{18}\) zetta \(10^{21}\) yotta \(10^{24}\) 3 bandwidth network bandwidth is a measurement indicating the maximum capacity of a wired or wireless communications link to transmit data over a network connection in a given amount of time.

item.title: linear algebra
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) this document needs some refactoring, some of the proofs may be incomplete 1 field 2 matrix a matrix is a rectangular array or table of numbers, symbols, or expressions, arranged in rows and columns, which is used to represent a mathematical object or a property of such an object a matrix can be constructed over a specific finite/infinite set of numbers for example \(\mathbb{Z}\) which is the set of integers, when we say a matrix is over a specific field of numbers we mean that the numbers in the matrix are limited to that field of numbers consider this matrix over the field \(\mathbb{Z}\), where all the numbers are integers \[\left[\begin{array}{rrrr} 1 & 1 & 0 & -24 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 & 11 \\ 3 & 0 & 0 & 3 \\ -1 & 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]\] 2.

item.title: linear algebra 2 sample exam 2
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) define the concept of a linear operator and \(\ker T\) where \(T\) is a linear operator let \(S,T:V 	o V\) be linear operators prove/disprove \(\ker S = \{0\} \implies \ker(T \circ S) = \{0\}\)  egin{gather*} S(\vec{v}) = \vec0 \implies \vec{v} = \vec0\ T(S(\vec{u})) = \vec0\ S(\vec{u}) = \vec0 \implies T(S(\vec{u})) = \vec0\ S(\vec{u}) 
eq \vec0 \centernot\implies T(S(\vec{u})) 
eq \vec0 \end{gather*}

item.title: rational root theorem
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) the rational root theorem states a constraint on rational solutions of a polynomial \(a_{n}x^{n}+a_{n-1}x^{n-1}+\cdots+a_{0} = 0\) with integer coefficients \(a_{i}\in \mathbb {Z}\) and \(a_{0}, a_{n}\neq 0\) the theorem states that each rational solution \(x = p⁄q\), written in lowest terms so that \(p\) and \(q\) are relatively prime, satisfies: \(p\) is an integer factor of the constant term \(a_0\), and \(q\) is an integer factor of the leading coefficient \(a_n\).

item.title: mental health
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 mental illness unlike physical illness, mental illness is related to problems that start in the brain. the brain is an organ. just like any other organs in our body, it can experience changes (healing or injury) based on life experiences like stress, trauma, rejection, lack of sleep, nutrition… although Psychiatry has advanced so much over the years to provide us with the knowledge and tools to deal with mental illness, nothing, not even science or religion can give us a better understanding of mental illness than art, thats why i will mostly focus on art rather than on the study of Psychology or Psychiatry 1.

item.title: probability
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) probability is a function, denoted by \(P\) that describes the probability for a specific incident to occur The probability of an impossible event is zero; the probability of a certain event is one. therefore, for any event \(A\), the range of possible probabilities is \(0 \le P(A) \le 1\) for \(S\) the sample space of all possibilities, \(P(S) = 1\). that is the sum of all the probabilities for all possible events is equal to one.

item.title: symacs
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) symacs, which stands for symbolic emacs is a latex-based interface to sympy built with emacs integration in mind symacs looks for common math writing patterns and parses them into sympy objects to then evaluate them symbolically 1 motivation for a latex-based computer algebra system interface 2 sage vs sympy sagemath is far sure much more powerful than sympy, but sympy is just a python library (about 10mb) and sagemath packs lots of software (including sympy and maxima) that amount to gigabytes of space, so i decided to just go with sympy, which hopefully will be much more powerful in the future as it has a huge community 3 parsing latex math expressions texsoup does all of the work from TexSoup import TexSoup mytex = r'$E_1=\pmqty{1 & 0\\ 0 & 1},E_2=\pmqty{0 & 1\\ 1 & 0},E_3=\pmqty{1 & 0\\ 0 & -1},E_4=\pmqty{0 & 1\\ -1 & 0}$' soup = TexSoup(mytex) print(soup.

item.title: gpg
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) gnupg is an encryption tool 1 encrypt file with password gpg -o <output-file> -c <file-to-encrypt> the argument -o takes the path to the output file (the encrypted one) and the flag -c tells gpg that we want to encrypt, you will be prompted to enter a password 2 decrypt file with password gpg -o <output-file> -d <file-to-decrypt> the argument -o takes the path to the output file (the decrypted one) and the flag -d tells gpg that we want to decrypt 3 encrypt directory with password to encrypt a directory, we simply compress it into a file: tar -cvf archive.

item.title: programming in C#
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) this document contains my notes on gui programming in C# 1 C# in emacs’ org-mode the following code allows running C# code in org-babel, given you have mono installed (require 'ob) (require 'csharp-mode) (add-to-list 'org-babel-tangle-lang-exts '("csharp" . "cs")) (defvar org-babel-default-header-args:csharp '()) (defun ob-csharp--build-script-run-command (path) "Create run command according to the PATH." (message path) (format "csc %s -r:System.Windows.Forms.dll -r:System.Drawing.dll >/dev/null; mono $(basename %s); rm *.

item.title: data structures
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) for most data structures i havent implemented deletion yet.. also its good to note that most of the code isnt optimized to hell, but it doesnt really matter since its only for education purposes and you should probably be using the data structures provided by the programming language’s standard library 1 properties of floor for every 2 normal numbers \(m,n\) and every real number \(x\) \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \] \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \] the following is true for every normal number \(n\) and any number \(x\) \begin{gather} \floor*{x}+n = \floor*{x+n} \qquad \ceil*{x}+n=\ceil*{x+n}\\ n-\floor*{x} = \ceil*{n-x} \qquad n-\ceil*{x} = \floor*{n-x}\\ \floor*{x} \geq \ceil*{x}-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} \leq \floor*{x}+1\\ \floor*{x} > x-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} < x+1\\ \floor*{x} \leq x \qquad \ceil*{x} \geq x\\ \floor*{x} = n \iff n \leq x < n+1 \qquad \ceil*{x} = n \iff n-1 < x \leq n \end{gather}

item.title: emacs
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rank}{rank}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 query syntax of a char (char-syntax (string-to-char "-")) 2 ob-async with latex this seems to be the only way to use ob-async with latex blocks: #+HEADER: :fit yes :imagemagick yes #+HEADER: :packages '("\\usepackage{tikz}") #+BEGIN_SRC latex :file /tmp/myimg.png :results file graphics :async \tikz\draw (0,0) circle (10px); \tikz\draw[fill=gray!30!white] (0,0) ellipse (28pt and 20pt); \tikz\draw[line width=2mm] (0,0) arc (30:200:1cm); \tikz\fill[color=red] (0,0) rectangle (2,1); #+END_SRC 3 reset variable to its default value (setq foo (eval (car (get 'foo 'standard-value)))) 4 xenops 4.

item.title: art
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 Edvard Munch 1.1 the scream the famous the scream painting: 1.2 despair 2 deimonnus deimonnus is the name of some artist’s blog i think is related to dsbm 3 Adam Riches 4 Raoof Haghighi “take them and leave me alone” 5 u/SalvaMX “if mental disorders were creatures” by u/SalvaMX 6 unknown mostly from reddit

item.title: discrete math
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 set 1.1 disjoint sets two sets are said to be disjoint sets if they have no element in common 1.2 multiset a multiset is a modification of the concept of a set that, unlike a set, allows for multiple instances for each of its elements. the number of instances given for each element is called the multiplicity of that element in the multiset 2 ordered pair given \(a, b\) are mathematical objects, \((a,b)\) is an ordered pair, \((a,b) \neq (b,a)\), as an example, the \((x,y)\) coordinates on a function is an ordered pair 3 cartesian product cartesian product of 2 sets is the set of all the possible ordered pairs that can be obtained by taking an element from \(A\) as the first in the pair and an element from \(B\) as the second \begin{gather*} A \times B = \{(a,b) \ | \ a \in A \land b \in B\}\\ (a,b) \in A \times B \Leftrightarrow a \in A \land b \in B \end{gather*}

item.title: fractals
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) fractals are a great way to explore mathematical concepts, here i try to explore as many as i can and i guide you through it all step by step 1 mandelbrot 1.1 characteristics The Mandelbrot set is the set of values of c in the complex plane for which the orbit of the critical point \(z_{n+1} = {z_n}^2 + c\) remains bounded a complex number c is a member of the Mandelbrot set if, when starting with \(z_0 = 0\) and applying the iteration repeatedly, the absolute value of \(z_n\) remains bounded for all \(n > 0\) For example, for \(c = 1\), the sequence is \(0, 1, 2, 5, 26, \ldots\), which tends to infinity, so 1 is not an element of the Mandelbrot set.

item.title: music
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rank}{rank}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) my favorite form of art 1 metal 1.1 post metal 1.1.1 Cult of Luna Cult of Luna are the kings of this subgenre, their most recent album (at the time of writing - <2022-05-06 Fri 18:24:00>) is an absolute masterpiece The Long Road North - Cult of Luna 1.2 melodic death/black - land of eargasm 1.2.1 Aetherian this album is pure eargasm, this band is basically Insomnium but better and heavier The Untamed Wilderness - Aetherian 1.

item.title: hugo
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rank}{rank}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) 1 redirect 404 to homepage to do this we create a file 404.html in our theme’s layout directory with the following content: {{ define "content"}} <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0; url=https://blog.mahmoodsheikh.com" /> <main id="main"> <div> <!-- for older browsers that cant handle redirects --> <h1 id="title"><a href="{{ "/" | relURL }}">Go Home</a></h1> </div> </main> {{ end }} hugo by default wont use this file, i use nginx so to make the webserver return this file on 404 errors we add the line error_page 404 /404.

item.title: tikz
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) for reference: refcard tikz is used to draw graphics in latex documents it can be imported like this: \usepackage{tikz} 1 primitive shapes drawing primitive shapes like lines/rectangles is very simple 1.1 lines \tikz\draw (0,0) -- (3,2); here |- generates a broken line while -- generates a straight line \tikz\draw (0,0) -- (3,2) -- (5,0) |- (4,3); \tikz\draw[->] (-2,0) -- (-3,0); 1.2 polygons tikz has lots of builtin shapes like: \tikz\draw (0,0) circle (10px); \tikz\draw[fill=gray!

item.title: vim
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rank}{rank}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) 1 global command 1.1 delete matching lines :g/pattern/d 1.2 run macro on matching lines :g/pattern/normal @q

item.title: homework 8
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) all the code ive written and used can be found on my blog given the sequence \(3,2,7,9,5,4,3.5,4.5,3.2,4.7,1,10,11,10.5,2.5,3.7,12\) insert the sequence into a binary search tree step by step #include <iostream> int main() { const float seq[] = {3,2,7,9,5,4,3.5,4.5,3.2,4.7,1,10,11,10.5,2.5,3.7,12}; BinarySearchTree<float> t; for (int i = 0; i < 17; ++i) { std::cout << "inserting " << seq[i] << std::endl; t.insert((int)(seq[i]*10), seq[i]); print_latex_forest(t.

item.title: Algebraic multiplicity
item.description: see https://people.math.carleton.ca/~kcheung/math/notes/MATH1107/wk10/10_algebraic_and_geometric_multiplicities.html#:~:text=The%20algebraic%20multiplicity%20of%20an,%5B1201%5D.

item.title: Arithmetic progression
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) An arithmetic progression or arithmetic sequence is a sequence of numbers such that the difference between the consecutive terms is constant if the first number of an arithmetic progression is \(a_{1}\) and the difference of successive numbers is \(d\) then the formula for the $n$th number in the sequence is: \[ a_{n} = a_1 + (n-1)d \] and the general formula is \[ a_{n} = a_m + (n-m)d \] where \(a_m\) is a number in the sequence at index \(m\)

item.title: Cpp
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 cpp errors 1.1 Segmentation fault the infamous Segmentation fault error is caused by accessing or attempting to operate on an illegal memory address 1.2 stack variables be careful when passing a pointer to a variable stored on the stack, e.g. the following program would fail because before the program exits the tree tries to delete its nodes but we would get a double free or corruption (out) error because its not safe to use delete on stack variables enum Color { white, red }; int main() { BinarySearchTree<Color> t; BinaryTreeNode<Color> n1(20,white); BinaryTreeNode<Color> n2(18, white); BinaryTreeNode<Color> n3(26, red); t.

item.title: Divide-and-conquer algorithm
item.description: divide and conquer is an algorithm design paradigm. A divide-and-conquer algorithm recursively breaks down a problem into two or more sub-problems of the same or related type, until these become simple enough to be solved directly.

item.title: doom metal
item.description: a subgenre of metal

item.title: emacs pinky
item.description: i know exactly how it feels when your pinky (leftmost finger) hurts from pressing the left control key often when using emacs to solve this i decided to map the right alt key to the ctrl key, to do that on linux i used the following command setxkbmap -option ctrl:ralt_rctrl you can put this line in your ~/.zprofile or ~/.xinitrc and youll be fine to press the right alt key (which is now mapped to ctrl) i use my right thumb, problem solved.

item.title: Euler's Number
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) Euler’s Number is a constant defined as \[ e = \lim_{x\to \pm\infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x \]

item.title: Evadne
item.description: one of my favorite doom metal bands

item.title: geometric progression
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) a geometric progression is a sequence of non-zero numbers where each term after the first is found by multiplying the previous one by a fixed, non-zero number called the common ratio the formula for the $n$th number in a geometric sequence with initial value \(a\) and common ratio \(r\) is: \[ a_{n}=ar^{n-1} \]

item.title: hebrew in latex
item.description: you need to install the package texlive-most, if you havent already this is the only approach that worked for me on arch linux install the texlive-langextra package with: sudo pacman -S texlive-langextra use the following basic template: \documentclass[12pt]{article} \usepackage[english,bidi=basic]{babel} %bidi=default with xelatex \babelprovide[import,main]{hebrew} \babelfont{rm}[Language=Default]{Latin Modern Roman} \babelfont[hebrew]{rm}{DejaVu Sans} \begin{document} \begin{center} \Huge \textbf{תקציר} \end{center} ניסוי ותהייה, או ניסוי וטעיה? \selectlanguage{english} some text \end{document} compile the file using lualatex with lualatex file.

item.title: javascript
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\)

item.title: latex
item.description: latex is the most featureful environment available to this day to create documents and to typeset math

item.title: lualatex
item.description: luatex is a successor to pdftex just like xelatex to run lualatex we use the command lualatex instead of pdflatex

item.title: metal
item.description: see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_metal_music

item.title: networking homework 2
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) the probability of a successful transmission of a packet in the first link is 0.56 the probability of a successful transmission of a packet in the second link is 0.24 a packet is delivered in both links for it to arrive successfully at the destination we have 10 packets to transmit find the probability of 3/10 successful transmissions of packets in both links the odds of a successful transmission is \(0.

item.title: operating systems
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) 1 operating systems todos DONE 1.0.1 homework 1 State “DONE” from “TODO” [2022-08-07 Sun 18:57:00] State “TODO” from [2022-08-07 Sun 18:57:00] 2 write struct to file #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> struct person { int id; char fname[20]; char lname[20]; }; int main() { FILE *outfile; // open file for writing outfile = fopen("person.dat", "w"); if (outfile == NULL) { fprintf(stderr, "
Error opened file
"); exit(1); } struct person input1 = {1, "rohan", "sharma"}; struct person input2 = {2, "mahendra", "dhoni"}; // write struct to file fwrite(&input1, sizeof(struct person), 1, outfile); fwrite(&input2, sizeof(struct person), 1, outfile); if(fwrite !

item.title: operating systems homework 1
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <unistd.h> #include <fcntl.h> struct phone { char first_name[20]; char last_name[20]; char phone_num[15]; }; const unsigned int PHONE_SIZE = sizeof(struct phone); void print_struct(int infile) { struct phone input; read(infile, &input, PHONE_SIZE); printf("First name: %s
Last name: %s
Phone number: %s
", input.first_name, input.last_name, input.phone_num); } int main() { int infile; infile = open("phones.dat", O_RDONLY); if (infile == -1) { perror("
error opening file
"); exit(1); } print_struct(infile); lseek(infile, PHONE_SIZE, SEEK_CUR); print_struct(infile); int filesize = lseek(infile, 0, SEEK_END); lseek(infile, filesize-PHONE_SIZE, SEEK_SET); print_struct(infile); lseek(infile, filesize-2*PHONE_SIZE, SEEK_SET); print_struct(infile); close(infile); return 0; } First name: Alice Last name: Chebba Phone number: 973-555-2015 First name: Jeff Last name: Goldberg Phone number: 201-555-3378 First name: Tony Last name: Iannino Phone number: 973-555-1295 First name: Susan Last name: Goldberg Phone number: 201-555-2087

item.title: scalar
item.description: basically a constant (non-variable) number

item.title: sum of arithmetic sequence
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) the sum of the numbers in a finite Arithmetic sequence is given by the following formula: \[ S = \frac{n(a_1 + a_n)}{2} \]

item.title: sum of geometric progression
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) the sum of a finite geometric progression with initial term \(a\), common ratio \(r\) and \(n\) terms can be found using the formula: \[ S_n = \frac{a(1-r^n)}{1-r} \]

item.title: tilde in latex
item.description: in latex tilde can be written in math mode using $\sim$ and in text mode using \texttildelow a raised tilde can be produced using \~ and \textasciitilde in text mode

item.title: trackify
item.description: a service similar to lastfm, written for spotify, available at https://trackifyapp.net trackify todos TODO fix the frontpage where some artist images dont load TODO the caching daemon exhausts the cpu, maybe increase the caching interval or improve the code or both

item.title: vuiet
item.description: a promising music player for emacs located at https://github.com/mihaiolteanu/vuiet you need a lastfm account and api credentials to be able to use this you need to put your api key and other information in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.lastfmrc note that inorder for functions that use emacs’ builtin browse-url to work you might need to customize the variable browse-url-browser-function depending on which browser you use, for me it was chrome so i used (setq browse-url-browser-function 'browse-url-chrome)

item.title: Whirr
item.description: my favorite shoegaze band

item.title: xelatex
item.description: xelatex is a successor of standard latex as it provides better unicode/font support to use it we run the command xelatex instead of pdflatex on a .tex file on arch linux fonts like xits are available in the texlive-fontsextra package although on my arch linux installation, xelatex couldnt locate the fonts on its own but lualatex could, so thats what i ended up using
